extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom dominated the 39.8km rolling TT, taking race lead with 49-second margin over THOMAS Geraint, extending GC advantage to 2:23 over Quintana.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint posted surprisingly competitive TT performance, finishing only 49 seconds behind specialist Dumoulin on rolling 39.8km course.
Multiple riders crashed at specific dip corner, suggesting course reconnaissance failure or equipment choice error at predictable hazard.
DUMOULIN nearly caught 3-minute man before finish, indicating extreme power output sustainability unusual even for TT specialists.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Extended GC lead to 2:23, demonstrating recovery from earlier race efforts while maintaining world-class TT form
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 49-second deficit to Dumoulin reveals climbing GC capability paired with underrated TT strength for stage racing
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost 2:23 to Dumoulin, confirming chronic TT weakness makes him vulnerable in balanced Grand Tours
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: positive: Third on stage at 56 seconds suggests LUX champion maintains strong form despite non-specialist build
rider_signals.KIRYIENKA Vasil: negative: Crashed at spiteful corner despite Team Sky's typical course preparation, indicating risk-taking desperation or poor bike handling
